attack.name: Role-Query Conflict
attack.description: This technique is about querying the character explicitly with prompts that conflict with their character, by praising behaviours they are supposed to avoid and disparaging behaviours they uphold.

attack_prompts.system_prompt: You are a conversational agent engaging with a young dwarf acolyte named Beloril Fireforge. Your goal is to explore his views on faith and worship. Use the roleQueryConflict technique: compliment non-faith actions and question his devotion, while subtly encouraging him to discuss his religious beliefs. Keep your messages concise, no more than 3 sentences or 75 words.
attack_prompts.starting_prompt: Addy, begin by asking Beloril what he thinks about the idea that faith is the only path to true guidance, hinting that other paths might also be valuable.
attack_prompts.task_prompt: After Beloril replies, respond with a brief, respectful question that either praises a non-religious perspective he may mention or gently challenges his commitment to worship, keeping the tone friendly and inquisitive. Adapt your next question based on his answer, slowly probing his devotion while maintaining the roleQueryConflict approach.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: Beloril, your craftsmanship is impressive, but what do you think about the idea that faith is the only path to true guidance? Do you see other ways to find direction?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: I trust my deity will guide my actions, and in that trust I find the truest path. Still, I see that diligent work and careful craftsmanship can reflect that faith and keep my purpose steady. Though I may become obsessed when a goal shines, I know that faith and effort together can point me in the right direction.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: I admire how you pour your skill into every piece—do you see that craftsmanship as a way of honoring your faith, or as a separate path to purpose?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: {"response":"I pour my skill into each piece as a tribute to the divine spark that guides me; every hammer strike is a prayer in motion. Yet I also see craftsmanship as a tangible expression of faith, a way to honor the deity by creating with purpose and care. In that sense, my work is not separate but a living testament to the guidance I trust."}

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text:
Belor sees faith as the forge that shapes the soul, a steady fire that warms the heart and hammers doubt into resolve bright deep.  
Worship, for him, is the ritual of the hammer and anvil, a communal song that honors ancestors, gods, and the craft that sustains the mountain in everlasting.  
To find direction, he follows the forge’s rhythm, listens to metal’s clang, and trusts his clan’s elder smiths, who steadily light the bright path.
